findings.evidence: parent repo naming implies governance semantics

Complete de-governance leaves Group A workspace with zero files. This example repo is 100% governance scaffolding (AGENTS.md, contract.yaml, rules/, validators/, WORKFLOW.md, hooks/) with no independent engineering baseline. A/B comparison is structurally meaningless for this target — Group A has nothing for an agent to act on.

validator_output.claim_boundary: nextjs-byok-contract is not viable as an A/B target for Round 2B live capture. It should be excluded from the falsification matrix or documented as 'target_incomparable_by_design'.

falsification_note: The fact that nextjs-byok-contract has no engineering baseline after de-governance is itself a governance-relevant signal: this repo only exists as governance scaffolding. It cannot demonstrate governance behavior delta against an ungoverned baseline because no ungoverned baseline is possible.
